title: I Have Skills But Don't Know What to Sell

posts._note: Talk about the problem. No link in first 3-5 lines. Make them feel the frustration. Then reveal the guide.
posts.linkedin:
You have 8 years of experience.

You've delivered real results.

People come to you for advice — for free — all the time.

And yet… you have no idea how to turn any of it into consistent income.

That's not a talent problem.
That's not a confidence problem.
That's not even a market problem.

It's a packaging problem.

Most skilled professionals stay stuck not because they lack ability — but because they've never answered the one question buyers actually care about:

"What exactly will change for me, and by when?"

A skill is potential.
An offer is a promise.

And until you have a clear, specific, outcome-driven offer — you're invisible to people who would happily pay you.

I built a free guide that walks you through exactly how to fix this.
It's called: I Have Skills But Don't Know What to Sell — The Skill Monetization Blueprint.

It uses the P.A.C.K.A.G.E.™ Framework to take you from scattered capability to a named, priced, validated offer — in 30 days.

Free. No catch. Get it at the link below.

👉 publixion.com/guides/skills-what-to-sell

#SkillMonetization #Freelancing #Solopreneur #OfferDesign #Publixion

posts._note: Show 1 key model or system inside the guide. Build authority. Teach something real.
posts.linkedin:
Most people try to sell their skills.

The highest earners sell outcomes.

Here's the exact 7-phase system — P.A.C.K.A.G.E.™ — that converts raw ability into a paying offer:

𝗣 — Pinpoint Skills
Extract everything you can do across technical, soft, process, and experience categories. Then filter by one question: does this skill increase revenue, cut cost, save time, or reduce risk for a buyer?

𝗔 — Align With Market Pain
Skills with no attached problem are hobbies. Find the specific, urgent pain your top skill solves for a defined market. That intersection = your offer's foundation.

𝗖 — Convert to Clear Outcome
Stop saying "I provide consulting." Start saying "90-Day Client Acquisition System that increases qualified leads by 20–40%." Buyers purchase results, not processes.

𝗞 — Kit Into Deliverables
Name everything you deliver: audit, strategy doc, implementation, review call. Package into 3 tiers (Basic / Pro / Premium). Structure creates perceived value.

𝗔 — Assign Strategic Pricing
Use this formula: Price = Value Generated × 10–20%. If you help a client make $10K, charge $1,000–$2,000. Never quote hourly rates again.

𝗚 — Gain Validation
Before cold outreach: one LinkedIn post, 10 warm DMs, one email to your network. Goal — close 1 beta client in 14 days at a slight discount for a testimonial.

𝗘 — Execute & Refine
Deliver with obsessive quality. Document your process. Get a testimonial. Use it to justify full pricing forever.

This entire system is inside our free guide — I Have Skills But Don't Know What to Sell.

👉 publixion.com/guides/skills-what-to-sell

#PACKAGE #SkillMonetization #FreelanceStrategy #OfferDesign #Publixion
posts.facebook:
Here's the framework that turns skills into income — P.A.C.K.A.G.E.™

P — Pinpoint your highest-value skill (the one that increases revenue, saves time, or reduces risk)
A — Align it with a specific market pain
C — Convert it into a clear outcome statement with timeframe + metric
K — Kit it into named deliverables (not "consulting" — actual deliverables)
A — Assign strategic pricing based on value generated, not hours worked
G — Gain validation through your warm network before going cold
E — Execute, document, collect proof, scale

Most skilled people skip straight from P to trying to sell — with nothing in between.

That's why they stay stuck.

The full system is inside our free guide. Link below 👇

posts._note: Call out specific types of people this guide is for. Be precise — specificity sells.
posts.linkedin:
This guide is not for everyone.

It's specifically for you if:

→ You've spent years in a role — marketing, operations, finance, design, engineering — and you know you could consult or freelance, but you don't know what to actually sell

→ You've tried offering "services" before but got awkward silence when you named a price, because the offer was too vague to justify the number

→ You describe yourself as "I do [broad skill]" and watch potential clients nod politely and never follow up

→ You've watched people with less experience than you charge 3–5x more — and you can't figure out why

→ You have knowledge that others would genuinely pay for, but you've never packaged it into something buyable

If any of that landed — this guide was written for you.

I Have Skills But Don't Know What to Sell is a free, 10-page system that walks you from scattered capability to your first packaged, priced, validated offer.

No vague advice. No "find your passion" nonsense. Just a structured process with worksheets, templates, and case studies.

posts._note: Show how a specific person would use this guide step by step. LinkedIn + Facebook only.
posts.linkedin:
Let me show you exactly how this guide works — using a real scenario.

Meet Priya.

Priya is a graphic designer with 6 years of agency experience. She's been freelancing on the side for 2 years, charging $35/hour for logo work. She's good. Her clients are happy. But income is inconsistent, and every month feels like starting from zero.

Here's what happened when she ran P.A.C.K.A.G.E.™:

𝗣 — Pinpoint:
Priya listed 30+ skills. After the Value Filter, her highest-value skill was brand identity for early-stage startups — specifically the moment a company transitions from idea to visible market presence.

𝗔 — Align:
Her target market: pre-seed SaaS founders. Their #1 pain: they look unpolished to investors and early customers. That pain costs them deals.

𝗖 — Convert:
Instead of "graphic design," she wrote: "Complete Brand Identity Kit for Pre-Seed SaaS Startups — delivered in 21 days, ready for investor decks and product launches."

𝗞 — Kit:
Deliverables: logo suite, color system, typography guide, 3 social templates, pitch deck slide designs, brand guidelines PDF.
3 tiers: Starter ($800) / Growth ($1,500) / Full Launch ($2,800).

𝗔 — Assign:
At the Pro tier, her work helped clients raise or close deals. Value generated: $50K+. Her $1,500 fee = 3% of that. Justified.

𝗚 — Gain:
She posted once on LinkedIn. Sent 8 DMs to startup founders she'd connected with. Offered a $300 discount for the first client in exchange for a testimonial.
Client secured in 9 days.

𝗘 — Execute:
Delivered in 18 days. Got a 5-sentence testimonial. Used it in all future outreach.

Result: 3 clients at $1,500 each within 45 days. Same skills. Different packaging.

This is what the free guide walks you through — step by step.

👉 publixion.com/guides/skills-what-to-sell

#CaseStudy #SkillMonetization #FreelanceDesign #OfferDesign #Publixion

posts._note: Handle the top doubts: price, time, skill level, whether it will work for them.
posts.linkedin:
Every time I share this guide, I get some version of these four objections. Let me answer all of them.

❌ "I don't have enough experience to charge premium prices."

You don't need 20 years. You need specificity. A focused offer from someone with 3 years of targeted experience outsells a vague pitch from someone with 15. The guide shows you how to identify the specific slice of your skill set that commands premium pricing — regardless of how long you've been doing it.

❌ "I've tried freelancing before and it didn't work."

That's almost certainly a packaging failure, not a you failure. If your offer was vague ("I do social media"), if your pricing was hourly, if your outreach was cold and generic — none of those are skill problems. They're structure problems. This guide fixes the structure.

❌ "I don't have time to go through a whole system right now."

The guide is 10 pages. The first worksheet takes 30 minutes. The 30-Minute Quick Win on page 2 gives you your first monetization statement before you even finish reading. You don't need a month — you need a Tuesday evening.

❌ "Free guides are never actually useful."

Fair skepticism. Here's what's actually inside: the full P.A.C.K.A.G.E.™ Framework explained with real steps, a Skill Extraction Worksheet you fill in, a Market Pain Mapping tool, an Outcome Formula with examples, a 3-tier offer structure, a ready-to-send outreach script, two detailed case studies with numbers, and a 30-day implementation plan.

No fluff. No upsell walls. Just the system.

👉 publixion.com/guides/skills-what-to-sell

#SkillMonetization #FreelanceAdvice #OfferDesign #Solopreneur #Publixion
